hook.text: Smokers are 20 times more likely to get lung cancer. That '20 times' is an odds ratio.

buildup.text: The odds ratio compares the odds of an outcome in exposed vs. unexposed groups.

discovery.text: An OR of 1 means no association. Above 1 means higher risk. Below 1 means protective.

twist.text: For rare diseases, the odds ratio approximates relative risk. For common conditions, it exaggerates.

climax.text: Odds ratios dominate medical research because they work with case-control studies where risk can't be measured.

punchline.text: How much more likely? The odds ratio answers that.

quiz.question: When does the odds ratio exaggerate the relative risk?
options: When the outcome is common — for rare outcomes they are approximately equal
When the sample size is very large
